evals.prompt: Create a complete HTML page that uses Dynamic Web TWAIN from CDN to scan documents. I need: a Scan button, an Upload button that uploads as PDF, a rotate button, and a button to convert the image to black and white. Make it work out of the box.
evals.expected_output: A complete, self-contained HTML file with CDN-based DWT setup including scan, upload-as-PDF, rotate, and B&W conversion functionality.
expectations: Uses CDN script tag for dynamsoft.webtwain.min.js
Sets Dynamsoft.DWT.ProductKey to configure the license
Sets Dynamsoft.DWT.ResourcesPath to a CDN URL for static resources
Calls Dynamsoft.DWT.Load() to initialize the SDK
Registers OnWebTwainReady event for initialization callback
Creates a div container element for the DWT viewer
Uses SelectSourceAsync to let user choose a scanner
Uses AcquireImageAsync with scan parameters (e.g. PixelType, Resolution)
Upload implementation uses HTTPUpload with PDF image type (IT_PDF)
Uses RotateRight or RotateLeft for image rotation functionality
Uses ConvertToBW for black and white image conversion

evals.prompt: I'm building a React app and need to add document scanning with Dynamic Web TWAIN. Show me step by step how to install the npm package, configure it, and create a scanning component with a scan button and image viewer.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step React setup guide covering npm install, resource copying, DWT initialization in useEffect, cleanup on unmount, scan button, and viewer container div.
expectations: Mentions npm install dwt as the package installation step
Mentions copying static resources from node_modules/dwt/dist to public folder
Sets Dynamsoft.DWT.ResourcesPath to a local path (e.g. /dwt-resources)
Uses useRef to hold the DWT object reference across renders
Calls Dynamsoft.DWT.Load() inside a useEffect hook
Cleanup function in useEffect calls Unload() on component unmount
Renders a div element with id for the DWT viewer container
Implements a Scan button handler with SelectSourceAsync + AcquireImageAsync

evals.prompt: In my Dynamic Web TWAIN app, I need to know when each page finishes scanning so I can update a page counter in the UI. What events should I listen to and how do I register them? Show me the code.
evals.expected_output: Code example showing DWTObject.RegisterEvent('OnPostTransferAsync', callback) for per-page scan completion, including page counter update logic.
expectations: Mentions OnPostTransferAsync as the per-page post-scan event
Uses DWTObject.RegisterEvent() to register the event listener
Demonstrates using the callback parameter (outputInfo) to access scan data
Mentions OnBufferChanged as an additional event for buffer-level changes
Shows HowManyImagesInBuffer to get the total page count
Event registration is done after OnWebTwainReady callback fires
